description: Use when deciding whether to delegate to a subagent (Agent tool) vs do work inline, writing a subagent prompt, picking an agent type (general-purpose / Explore / Plan / custom), running parallel or background agents, designing a custom subagent in ~/.claude/agents/, or evaluating whether an orchestrator-with-specialists pattern fits the task. Routes to chapter 16 (subagents) of claude-spine.

results.query: I want to write a custom subagent in ~/.claude/agents/ for 'review my migration for safety' — walk me through the SKILL-like file
the deploy is failing with a subprocess error — debug the agent that's spawning the build
the user-agent string isn't being passed through the reverse proxy — fix the nginx config
I'm thinking 'orchestrator + specialists' for the codebase rewrite — one agent plans, several execute. Does that pattern fit here or am I over-engineering?
the AI agent we're integrating with is throwing 429s — handle the rate limit
spawn three parallel agents to refactor the auth helper, write the test suite, and update the README in one go
explain how Node.js child_process.fork creates subagents — wait, subprocesses
is this a job for the Explore agent or the general-purpose one — I'm looking for every file in the repo that imports 'supabase-js'
I need to audit 40 route handlers for missing zod validation — should I just grep, or is this where a subagent earns its keep?
design the database for our multi-tenant agent platform — agents and their tools
